architecture.summary: CoLLEGe is a meta-learning framework that generates task-general embeddings for new concept tokens in frozen autoregressive LLMs. Given a few example sentences containing a new token, the system: (1) encodes each support sequence with a frozen RoBERTa MLM and trainable Transformer encoder to extract contextual embeddings; (2) aggregates these via mean pooling to produce a single concept embedding; (3) projects this embedding into both input and output embedding spaces of a frozen LLaMA-2 LM via learned linear layers; (4) trains the projection layers by sampling support/query sequences from pretraining corpora, using next-word prediction loss combined with negative example sampling and knowledge distillation to match the frozen LM's output distributions on the query sequence.
trained_components: two linear projection layers (map aggregated concept embedding to LLM input and output spaces)

biblio.title: CoLLEGe: Concept Embedding Generation for Large Language Models

claims.statement: CoLLEGe achieves 49.3% accuracy on the GRE Verbal Reasoning task with a definition (D+4-shot), compared to 24.0% for Prompting and 10.6% for HiCE
CoLLEGe achieves 1065.57 ELO on definition generation, compared to 1032.28 for Prompting and 975.64 for HiCE
CoLLEGe achieves 17.81 ROUGE-L on definition generation, compared to 15.54 for Prompting and 7.98 for HiCE
CoLLEGe achieves 60.0% accuracy on the CoLLEGe-Slang benchmark with 4-shot examples, compared to 53.8% for Prompting and 29.0% for Additive
CoLLEGe performs all evaluations zero-shot without additional task-specific training or finetuning, unlike FLAN-T5 baselines which are specialized through instruction tuning
Knowledge distillation and negative example sampling, when combined, significantly boost performance; neither is effective on its own
The averaging mechanism cannot fully achieve parity with pretrained embeddings, even with more support sequences
Generated embeddings sometimes miss precise details in examples, instead encoding higher-level semantic information, visible in incorrect generated definitions
